item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
just checked my past comments to see if anyone actually replied, cool to actually get a reply from an author :)

Wanted to clarify that I didn't mean any harm with the -J comment, I think your distinction is spot on, but it just seemed too absurd for my personal taste. I just sort of did that "pffffsshhsssuure" thing while reading ^^

Really glad I was actually able to provide more helpful criticism though :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I reject any "this should be a -J comments" out of hand, since this isn't actually a funny article, just an absurdist one.

The point about the unidentified individual is a good one, since that was really just me as of two years ago trying to be spooky and ominous, but failing in the process. I've updated that line to say it was a GRU-P operative, which is the decision I had come to since writing. There's no reason not to say that in the main SCP.

EDIT: On second thought, I think I actually do remember what was up with that: it may have been part of an implication that the Foundation doesn't start talking with the dolphins until the 1980s (given that communications are based in Auslan, which didn't exist until then), and by then all the dolphins who talked to the individual are dead, and the dolphins left weren't able to describe them in sufficient detail? Either way, it's been a while and I forget, it does work better to just say it's a GRU-P agent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote. If this had a -J designation, maybe. Without it, this just seems awkward.

"The DCRI was educated in Marxist philosophy by an unidentified individual in 1940."

That seems important. And also begs the question why that individual is unidentified? Surely the dolphins would be able to provide at least some information. And if the Foundation wouldn't want whoever educated them to be identified, why would they even mention it in the first place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of dolphins creating nations and being at war is fantastic. I don’t take this seriously, but I don’t think I’m meant to. It’s silly and cute to me, and I love it. +1!!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Everything is better with Dolphins.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article itself is pretty swell, but we need to talk about that title my dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: OMG……Cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now octopus and crow have some competition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Communists dolphins in an anti-communist nation. Interesting.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This reminds of a funny website I once saw in a dark corner of the internet.
anti-dolphin.org

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's enough communities of sentient animal already on the site that it's not enough of a hook for me, and the Marxist spiders did the political angle first. Giving the dolphins the ability to actually wage war isn't enough of a hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Animal Farm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of my favourite SCPs. I’m not sure why, but a race of sentient dolphins seem really appealing to me. I thought that this was going to be a ridiculous SCP because of the start, but this turned out very well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SPC-1258 has some similarities actually.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I want an SPC crossover now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Flipper Marx

Just… take my upvote already. It was good to begin with, but THAT… I'd have upvoted even if the rest of it was bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cetacean, I think you mean? Selacian refers to sharks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can’t not upvote Celacian Realpolitick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: hail the great dolphins of the world! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Human mind cannot grasp what sentient dolphins hold!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: posadas is real and he is my friend

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I see the similarities between SCP-1006 and why that might drag this down, there a point of technicality in that these are not all communist dolphins, and even so the similarity here is tangential enough to not be a deal breaker for me.

I do appreciate the interplay between the different nations the dolphins have created, and honestly would love to read a tale about how exactly the dolphins intend to go to war with the Foundation.

+1. Not bad at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with this, with the added complaint that this doesn't do much for me in the originality department. Dolphins acting like or outsmarting humans is a pretty common idea (eg. Hitchhiker's Guide and even the Simpsons) and we already have communist animals (SCP-1006). Like you said, there isn't much to the characterisation of the dolphins that justifies the repetition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would strongly advise you to keep that opinion to yourself on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehh. This doesn't quite work for me. I definitely never would've thought of dolphin communists and commend you for your originality, but it seems like you just transferred human thoughts and emotions over to dolphins with a few minor variations. If you could make the characterization of the dolphins feel more alien and less familiar, I might like this more.

Not a fan of the addendum, honestly. I suppose I can buy the idea of the dolphins working together, but you took a lot of time to establish the Foundation's interactions with these groups and then suddenly tore apart those relations for shock value. There is a good idea, but unfortunately this is a -1 for me right now.

Edit: Also, as I said to Cimmerian before he changed his, these unnecessarily long titles grind my gears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
first man in space

first space station

first unmanned cargo freighter

first multi module space station

buran

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought the final note was a little silly. Otherwise, I like this. My initial expectation was this was the SPC-verse creeping into baseline, but there's actually nothing to disabuse me of that notion after all. :V

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, I like it, no additional comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, if we wanted to destroy them/keep them from advancing, it'd be a good plan

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Dolphin's Communist Republic of Indonesia:

What's with people indoctrinating animals into socialism?

item.title: JAMCON 2: TROPICAL -- The Dolphin Manifesto
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation itself, therefore, supplies the dolphins with its own elements of political and general education, in other words, it furnishes the dolphins with weapons for fighting the Foundation.

This is based off one of the first ideas I had for an SCP, which I decided to roll with for Jam Con!

Thanks to my reviewers: Zyn, GrimmCreeper, LordStonefish, Stallmantic, Gorinich, Othello

Attribution:
Source. This is the third image I've used for this SCP, but it's by far my favorite (and public domain).

The title's now a parody of a quote from the Communist Manifesto.

If you liked this thing and would like to read some comments about it, or check out other things I've written, head to my author page for more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love how you expanded on SCP-3477 using this skip.

+1
